untranslatable_reason: This signal requires multi-leg synthetic pricing analysis, bid-ask spread verification across paired instruments, and relative value arbitrage mechanics that cannot be expressed using single-instrument price/technical indicators. The core requirement is comparing an underlying instrument against its synthetic equivalent (e.g., stock vs call-put synthetic), calculating basis spreads, and monitoring liquidity on both legs simultaneously. No single-leg technical indicators in the allowed set can replicate synthetic pricing or cross-instrument basis analysis.
translation_notes: NATENBERG_DRY_100_0 is a conversion arbitrage signal requiring two-legged options/futures analysis. All threshold parameters ([THRESHOLD_NEEDED]) remain undefined in source material. The allowed indicator set lacks: (1) synthetic pricing models, (2) basis/spread tracking between paired instruments, (3) multi-leg liquidity metrics, (4) time-decay (theta) measurement. A functional implementation requires option pricing data, synthetic position construction, and relative valuation tools outside standard OHLCV technical analysis.
